steps.title: Place the underlined cause
steps.content: The question asks what the underlined portion does in the whole passage. That portion says Randall's broadsides became associated with the innovative Black Arts Movement. The next clause says Randall's format may likewise seem to have been pioneering, so the underlined information creates a possible impression that his publication format was new.

steps.title: Separate setup from correction
steps.content: Choice A describes the later correction that broadside poetry also flourished in seventeenth-century London, not the underlined portion itself. Choice C overstates the line as an explanation of the broadsides' importance to the Black Arts Movement. Choice D shifts to Randall's reason for publishing Black poets, which appears earlier. Choice B matches the underlined portion because it gives the reason a reader might mistakenly view Randall's use of broadsides as pioneering. The answer is B.
